description: An RFID Device Specialist at a logistics warehouse has bulk-loaded 500+ RFID tag-read events (in JSON format) from middleware into the Oracle table 'RFID_EVENTS' in the HR schema. The operations team needs structured views and a repeatable reporting pipeline. Create a relational view named 'RFID_EVENTS_FLAT' that extracts key fields (EVENT_ID, TAG_ID, EPC_URI, READER_ID, FACILITY, ZONE, READ_TIME, RSSI_DBM, SKU, ITEM_DESCRIPTION, ITEM_CATEGORY, UNIT_VALUE_USD, and EVENT_TYPE) from the JSON 'EVENT_DATA' column. Create an analytical view named 'ZONE_TRAFFIC_SUMMARY' that aggregates data by FACILITY and ZONE, showing TOTAL_READS, UNIQUE_TAGS, UNIQUE_SKUS, ARRIVAL_COUNT, DEPARTURE_COUNT, and AVG_SIGNAL_STRENGTH (rounded to 1 decimal). Develop a PL/SQL function 'GET_TAG_HISTORY(p_tag_id IN VARCHAR2) RETURN VARCHAR2' that returns a pipe-delimited string of events for a tag (format: 'ZONE:EVENT_TYPE:READ_TIME|...'), ordered by time, or 'NO_EVENTS_FOUND' if the tag doesn't exist. Export a report of the zone traffic summary with headers to /home/ga/Desktop/warehouse_report.txt. Connection: Host=localhost, Port=1521, Database=XEPDB1, User=hr, Password=hr123.
